message:
So yesterday our power went out for a few hours—nothing dramatic, just a blown transformer down the street. But I realized we actually had flashlights *and batteries in the same drawer*, which sounds tiny until you're fumbling around in the dark wondering where anything is.

Turns out that one small thing—keeping basic supplies in one easy-to-find spot—made the whole situation feel manageable instead of chaotic. We had light, the kids weren't scared, and I didn't have to excavate the garage.

It's funny how preparedness doesn't have to be complicated. Sometimes it's just knowing where your stuff is.

What's one small thing you've done that surprised you by actually being useful when you needed it?
